title: Gloriously Crunchy Ethiopian Lamb Fir Fir
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring tender lamb and a crunchy texture, flavored with bitter sansho and a blend of spices, perfect for a special occasion. This keto-friendly recipe is sure to impress your guests.
instructions: Heat the ghee in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the berbere spice, mitmita, and ground sansho pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the lamb broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the lid and increase the heat to high, cooking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced and the lamb is crispy.
Stir in the chopped parsley and season with salt to taste.
Serve the lamb fir fir hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
